participate.title: Citizen Participation
participate.subtitle: The ZOE programme is driven by citizens as much as by the municipality. There are many ways to get involved — from sharing a local idea to volunteering in the field. Choose how you want to participate below.
participate.rewardsLink: Every action earns ZOE points — see the Rewards Programme
participate.prototypeNotice: Prototype: this form does not send data anywhere. Local success message only.
participate.deploymentNote: In a real deployment, your input would be sent to the ZOE coordination team for review.
participate.prototypeNote: Note: This is a prototype. No data was transmitted or stored.

participate.eventsSubtitle: Join a cleanup day, biodiversity workshop, or community forum in person.

accessibility.title: Accessibility Statement
accessibility.subtitle: ZOE Platform — Municipality of Northern Corfu
accessibility.conformanceText: This website partially conforms to WCAG 2.1 Level AA as required by EU Directive 2016/2102 and the European Accessibility Act (EAA, 2025). Some areas are still in active development as part of this research prototype.
accessibility.limitsText: As this is a research prototype (DSR artefact, FAU SoSe 2026), some features are not yet fully optimised for assistive technologies. Known limitations include participation option texts currently available in English only.
accessibility.feedbackText: We welcome feedback on the accessibility of this platform. If you experience any barriers, please contact us:
accessibility.legalBasisText: This accessibility statement is made pursuant to EU Directive 2016/2102 on the accessibility of websites and mobile applications of public sector bodies, transposed via EN 301 549.

hero.title: ZOE — Environmental Actions for Northern Corfu
hero.subtitle: A transparent, participatory platform connecting citizens with the environmental projects protecting the nature, coastlines, and communities of Northern Corfu.

stats.disclaimer: * All statistics are prototype dummy data for demonstration purposes.

pillars.heading: Why the ZOE Platform?
pillars.subheading: Environmental governance in small municipalities often lacks visibility and citizen connection. ZOE changes that with three core commitments.

transparency.title: Transparency
transparency.description: Every project publishes progress metrics, participation numbers, and impact data openly. Citizens can see where funding goes and what is being achieved.

participation.title: Citizen Participation
participation.description: From submitting ideas to volunteering on the ground, the ZOE platform connects residents directly to the environmental actions shaping their community.

impact.title: Sustainability Impact
impact.description: Aligned with the UN Sustainable Development Goals, each ZOE project is designed to deliver measurable, long-term environmental benefit.

featured.heading: Active Projects
featured.subheading: Environmental actions underway right now in Northern Corfu.

cta.heading: Your participation matters
cta.body: Whether you have five minutes to share feedback or five hours to volunteer at a restoration site — the ZOE programme needs your involvement to succeed.
cta.rewards: Every action earns ZOE points — rise from Σπόρος (Seed) to Θεματοφύλακας (Steward) and unlock recognition from the municipality.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: ZOE Platform — Municipality of Northern Corfu
privacy.prototypeNotice: This is a research prototype. No real personal data is collected or processed.
privacy.overviewText: The ZOE platform is a Design Science Research prototype developed for academic purposes (FAU SoSe 2026). It does not collect, store, or process any real personal data. All user accounts and platform data are fictional seed data used solely for demonstration purposes.
privacy.dataText: The prototype uses fictional seed data (names, email addresses, participation records) generated for testing purposes. Any data entered during the demonstration is stored locally in a development database and is not shared with third parties.
privacy.cookiesText: The platform uses localStorage to persist language preference and authentication state within the browser. No tracking cookies are set. No third-party analytics are used.
privacy.rightsText: Under GDPR, you have the right to access, rectify, and erase your data. As this is a prototype with fictional data, these rights are exercised by contacting the research team.

getInvolved.title: Get Involved
getInvolved.subtitle: Whether you live in Northern Corfu or are visiting for a week — there is a way for you to help protect it. Explore ZOE's initiatives, see how visitors can contribute, and stay informed.

initiatives.heading: Explore initiatives by theme
initiatives.intro: ZOE bundles the municipality's environmental actions into four thematic initiatives. Choose a theme to see the related actions.

marineCoast.name: Marine & Coast
marineCoast.description: Protecting Northern Corfu's 22 km coastline and freshwater streams — beach cleanups, marine litter monitoring and water quality.

natureBiodiversity.name: Nature & Biodiversity
natureBiodiversity.description: Safeguarding natural monuments — the Antinioti Lagoon, the Erimitis Peninsula and traditional olive landscapes.

circularClimate.name: Circular & Climate
circularClimate.description: Cutting waste and closing loops — recycling hubs and community composting across the villages.

educationTourism.name: Education & Tourism
educationTourism.description: Building awareness — environmental education in schools and a sustainable tourism scheme.

tourists.heading: How visitors can contribute
tourists.intro: Northern Corfu welcomes around four million visitors a year. Tourism is often seen only as a source of waste — but visitors can also be part of the solution.

cleanups.title: Join a beach cleanup
cleanups.desc: Spend an hour at a monthly cleanup in Kassiopi, Sidari or Palaiokastritsa.

report.title: Report litter or pollution
report.desc: Flag a litter hotspot or a pollution incident so the municipality can act.

choose.title: Choose certified operators
choose.desc: Favour accommodation and tours committed to ZOE's sustainable tourism standards.

respect.title: Respect nature & wildlife
respect.desc: Keep your distance from turtle nesting sites and protected wetlands.

tourists.evidenceNote: Evidence from comparable Mediterranean destinations shows that involving visitors and communities through digital tools measurably improves a destination's sustainability (Vegas Macias et al., 2023; Laksmi et al., 2026).

newsletter.heading: Stay informed
newsletter.intro: Get occasional updates on new actions, events and results in Northern Corfu.
newsletter.invalidEmail: Please enter a valid email address.
newsletter.success: Thanks! In the full version you would now receive a confirmation email.
newsletter.prototypeNotice: Prototype: no email is stored or sent. This illustrates the planned newsletter opt-in.

sdgDashboard.title: SDG Dashboard
sdgDashboard.intro: The ZOE programme aligns all projects to relevant UN Sustainable Development Goals. This dashboard shows which goals are addressed, how many projects contribute to each, and illustrative progress estimates.
sdgDashboard.prototypeNote: All percentages and figures are fictional dummy data for demonstration purposes only.
sdgDashboard.aboutText: SDG alignment is self-reported by project coordinators and reviewed by the ZOE team. Progress percentages represent the municipality's own assessment against locally-defined targets. This dashboard is a prototype — a real system would pull live data from verified project reports.
